surface.name: Elastic Cloud trial to first Elasticsearch Console search
surface.selection_basis: Workshop selection policy (2026-07-19): when official docs present peer routes, commit to the fastest and most commonly used documented route. Prefer the vendor's first-listed or recommended quickstart, dashboard/console over CLI when both are peers, and the route with an explicit vendor time claim or fewest documented steps when tied. Selected route: Elastic Cloud trial to first Elasticsearch Console search. The current Search getting-started page recommends Elastic Cloud Serverless for simplicity and speed and points to Index and search basics. This is a complete representative Search candidate, not a platform-wide choice over Observability or Security. Prior surface before policy commit: Elastic platform, solution and deployment type not selected.

alternatives_considered.name: Get started with Elasticsearch
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: This is the Search solution path, while Elastic presents Observability and Security as peer platform solutions.

alternatives_considered.name: Get started with Elastic Observability
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: This is the Observability solution path, with additional source and ingestion-mode branches.

alternatives_considered.name: Get started with Elastic Security
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: This is the Security solution path, not a platform-wide default.

alternatives_considered.name: Local Elasticsearch and Kibana in Docker
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: Elastic lists local development alongside Elastic Cloud Hosted and Serverless quick-start options without making it the platform-wide default.

entry_point.developer_intent: Get started with the Elastic platform, with no solution use case or deployment model supplied.
assumed_prior_state: The developer has arrived after discovery but has not selected Search, Observability, or Security.
The developer has not selected managed cloud, local Docker, or self-managed deployment.

documented_first_success.official_milestone: The selected Elasticsearch Console route demonstrates first success when the expected book appears under hits.hits with its original _source JSON.
documented_first_success.normalized_outcome: The expected book appears under hits.hits with its original _source JSON.
documented_first_success.observable_completion_signal: The expected book appears under hits.hits with its original _source JSON.

boundary_evidence.official_label_or_terminal_state: The expected book appears under hits.hits with its original _source JSON.

documented_first_success.why_this_is_the_boundary: Selected under workshop fastest/most-used policy; terminal taken from the committed official candidate route.

prerequisites.requirement: A selected need: build search, observe systems, or monitor and respond to security activity.
A selected deployment model: Elastic Cloud Serverless, Elastic Cloud Hosted, local Docker, self-managed, Elastic Cloud Enterprise, or Elastic Cloud on Kubernetes.

details: The overview maps different developer needs to Elasticsearch, Elastic Observability, and Elastic Security.
primary_path.success_signal: The solution comparison and Get started links are visible.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: No universal route continues without a use-case choice.

primary_path.action: Open Elastic Cloud Sign Up and register with email and password, Google, Microsoft, or a supported marketplace.
details: First signup creates an Elastic Cloud organization and starts a free 14-day trial.
An email address can belong to only one Elastic Cloud account and a user can belong to only one organization at a time.
primary_path.input: Eligible email identity or supported federated or marketplace identity.
primary_path.output: Elastic Cloud account, organization, and trial.
primary_path.success_signal: The Elastic Cloud home page is available.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: A reused account email or existing organization membership can require leaving the organization or using another email.

primary_path.action: From the Elastic Cloud home page, choose to create a serverless project and select the Elasticsearch project type.
details: The trial currently permits one active serverless project.
Project creation requires the admin predefined role or an equivalent custom role.
Elastic says to choose Elasticsearch when unsure because it provides core search capabilities and search-building UI tools.
primary_path.input: Elastic Cloud trial organization with project-creation permission.
primary_path.success_signal: The project settings can be reviewed and submitted.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Missing admin-equivalent permission, the active-project trial limit, the broader organization project limit, or blocked Elastic CDN domains can prevent creation or leave the UI blank.

primary_path.action: Review the serverless project settings and launch the project.
details: The public generic project page does not enumerate every current Elasticsearch settings field.
primary_path.input: Elasticsearch project selection and accepted settings.
primary_path.success_signal: Elastic creates the project and exposes entry to the project UI.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Invalid project settings or provisioning failures block project access; no duration is documented for this candidate.

primary_path.action: Open the Elasticsearch project and navigate to Dev Tools, Console.
details: Console can also be opened as Persistent Console from the footer on supported Search and Elasticsearch Serverless pages.
primary_path.output: Interactive Console request and response panels.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Blocked kibana.estccdn.com or cloud.elastic.co can make the project UI blank; Console can also be disabled by configuration.

primary_path.success_signal: The JSON response contains acknowledged true, shards_acknowledged true, and index books.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: A non-success HTTP status or JSON error means the index was not created.

primary_path.action: Run POST books/_doc with the documented Snow Crash JSON document.
details: The document includes name, author, release_date, and page_count.
primary_path.input: Created books index and sample JSON.
primary_path.success_signal: The response result is created, failed shard count is zero, and a document ID is returned.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Mapping, request-format, authorization, or shard failures prevent the document from becoming searchable.

primary_path.action: Make the indexed document available for search in near real time.
details: Elastic documents near-real-time search availability rather than immediate transactional visibility.
primary_path.success_signal: A subsequent search can return the document.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: A search issued before refresh may not immediately return the new document; no candidate-specific wait duration is given.

primary_path.success_signal: The response has timed_out false and a hits array containing the indexed book document.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: An empty result can mean refresh delay, wrong index, or a failed earlier indexing request; an error response must be corrected before retrying.

primary_path.action: Inspect the response panel and verify the returned hit contains the Snow Crash source fields.
details: Console also displays the HTTP status code and request execution time.
primary_path.output: Verified first Elasticsearch index, document, and search result.
primary_path.success_signal: The expected book appears under hits.hits with its original _source JSON.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Missing or different source data means the documented first-search terminal has not been reached.

branches.condition: The goal is to build a search experience.
branches.path: Follow Get started with Elasticsearch, then choose Serverless, Stack, or local Docker and continue through data ingestion and query construction.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Candidate success becomes a documented indexing and search result, but the guide still offers multiple search quickstarts.

branches.condition: The goal is infrastructure or application observability.
branches.path: Follow Get started with Elastic Observability, choose a project tier and telemetry source, collect data, then explore the relevant UI.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Candidate success becomes visible logs, metrics, or traces, with source-specific setup branches.

branches.condition: The goal is threat detection and response.
branches.path: Follow Get started with Elastic Security, create a project, ingest data through a selected integration, and continue to a use-case quickstart.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Candidate success becomes a Security-specific data or use-case outcome.

friction_gates.description: Elastic requires a solution choice among Search, Observability, and Security before the documented routes converge on any task.
Elastic also requires a deployment-model choice, with managed, local, and self-managed options carrying different account and environment gates.

sources.title: Get started with Elastic Observability
sections_used: Create an Elastic Observability Serverless project
Collect application traces, metrics, and logs
Explore your logs, metrics, and traces

sources.title: Get started with Elastic Security
sections_used: Get started with your use case

sources.title: Deployment options
evidence_supported: Cloud Hosted, Serverless, and local Docker quick-start choices

sources.title: Sign up and create an Elastic Cloud organization
sections_used: Maintain access to your trial projects and data
evidence_supported: One active Serverless project trial limit

sources.title: Run API requests with Console
evidence_supported: JSON, HTTP status, and timing response signals

uncertainties.question: Which Elastic solution defines the intended first-mile journey?
uncertainties.impact: It changes the actor, prerequisites, steps, gates, and completion signal.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: The official platform overview presents Elasticsearch, Observability, and Security as use-case-dependent peers.

uncertainties.question: Which deployment model should the selected solution use?
uncertainties.impact: It determines whether the journey begins with trial signup, project creation, Docker installation, Kubernetes, or self-managed infrastructure.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: The deployment guide offers multiple quick-start and advanced models without a platform-wide default.

excluded_after_success: Any Elasticsearch index or search quickstart
Any Security integration or detection setup
Scaling, retention, alerting, dashboards, and advanced analysis
